identified_paleocourse: paleo-Ghaggar-Hakra (Sirmur foothills to Rann of Kutch)
source_paper: Giosan et al. 2012 (PNAS 109:E1688); Clift et al. 2012 (Geology 40:211)
provenance: Centroid of the published reconstructed paleocourse running from the Shivalik foothills (NE terminus ≈ 30.5°N, 77.0°E) through Sirsa, Hanumangarh, Anupgarh in Rajasthan, then through the Cholistan Desert (Yazman, Derawar, Fort Abbas) in Pakistan to the Rann of Kutch (SW terminus ≈ 25.0°N, 70.0°E). Coordinates derived as the geometric midpoint of the published NE-SW extent; landmark points are standard reference locations cited in both Giosan and Clift 2012.

source_paper: Yashpal, Sahai, Sood & Agrawal 1980, Proc. Indian Acad. Sci. (Earth Planet. Sci.) 89(3):317-331; contrasted by Clift et al. 2012
provenance: Centroid of the pre-capture paleocourse proposed in the older satellite-based reconstruction of Yashpal et al. 1980, running from the HP foothills (≈31.5°N, 77.0°E) westward across the Punjab plain to join the paleo-Ghaggar near Suratgarh (≈29.3°N, 73.9°E). Note: Clift et al. 2012 argue that the major paleo-contribution was from a paleo-Yamuna (not paleo-Sutlej) and that this older hypothesis is not supported by U-Pb zircon provenance data. This row is provided for sensitivity analysis only; both alternative reconstructions are discussed in the paper's Appendix A.
